KYC Package POC Proposal
Reusable KYC Logic for capture-core · Architecture & Migration Plan
Objective
Create a reusable KYC React package in capture-core so KYC logic does not need to be rewritten in
every project. The package will be consumed by capture-remap and future applications.
Background
Today, the KYC implementation pattern across projects is:
•capture-core contains core logic
•capture-remap handles UI
•Parts of KYC flow are repeated or tightly coupled to project-specific code
After  reviewing  the  KYC  implementation  in  stocko-web-app,  the  KYC  journey  is  already  structured
around  a  multi-step  flow,  but  the  current  implementation  mixes  business  logic,  UI  rendering,  routing,
Redux state handling, modal/toast behavior, analytics, and third-party redirect handling — making reuse
difficult across repositories.
Current KYC Journey

and third-party redirect handling — making reuse
difficult across repositories.
Current KYC Journey
The current KYC module includes these major steps:
1
Email OTP
2
Mobile OTP
3
KRA / PAN verification
4
DigiLocker flow
5
Personal information
6
Nominee details
7
Bank verification
8
Trading preferences
9
Income proof / Account Aggregator
10
Subscription plan
11
Selfie verification
12
Signature upload
13
E-sign

14
Completion / resume / rejected handling
Problem Statement
The current KYC implementation is hard to reuse because the main orchestration hook contains almost
everything:
•API interactions
•Navigation decisions
•Next/previous step logic
•Session/local storage handling
•Guest user handling
•Bank verification retry logic
•DigiLocker and E-sign redirects
•Analytics and modal side effects
As a result:
•The same logic risks being copied into multiple repos

nalytics and modal side effects
As a result:
•The same logic risks being copied into multiple repos
•UI and business logic are not cleanly separated
•Future maintenance cost increases
•Onboarding new projects takes longer
POC Goal
Build  a  reusable  KYC  package  in  capture-core  that  centralizes  the  KYC  business  flow  and  can  be
plugged into capture-remap without rewriting the logic.
The  package  should  be:  reusable  ·  headless  /  logic-first  ·  UI-agnostic  ·  routing-agnostic  ·
configurable per project
Proposed Solution
Move the KYC flow engine into capture-core and keep UI implementation in capture-remap.
capture-core = KYC brain   |   capture-remap = KYC presentation layer
What Goes Into capture-core
•KYC step constants, status constants, sub-screen constants
•Shared types / interfaces
•Validation rules & payload builders
•Step transition logic & flow resume logic

types / interfaces
•Validation rules & payload builders
•Step transition logic & flow resume logic
•API client contract
•Reusable hooks: useKycFlow
•Redirect response handlers for DigiLocker / E-sign

What Stays in capture-remap
•Page routing, screen layout
•Forms and field presentation
•Modals, toasts, branding/theme
•Stepper / header design
•Project-specific analytics wiring
Recommended Package Design
The package should be headless-first. Instead of exporting fully built pages, it exports logic primitives:
•KycProvider
•useKycFlow
•useKycState
•kycReducer / internal state store
•kycConstants
•kycValidators
•kycApiAdapter
Suggested Folder Structure
capture-core/
  src/
    kyc/
      constants/     ← step ids, statuses, screen constants
      types/         ← request/response & internal state types
      validators/    ← PAN, DOB, bank, nominee, trading prefs

quest/response & internal state types
      validators/    ← PAN, DOB, bank, nominee, trading prefs
      api/           ← reusable KYC service wrapper
      adapters/      ← router, storage, analytics (injectable)
      state/         ← reducer, actions, default state
      hooks/         ← useKycFlow, useKycNavigation, useKycResume
      provider/      ← top-level KYC context/provider
      index.ts
Adapter-Based Design
To  keep  the  package  reusable,  project-specific  behavior  is  passed  in  through  adapters.  The  package
emits events; the consuming app decides how to respond:
•storage adapter
•navigation adapter
•analytics adapter
•notification adapter
•modal adapter
•API adapter

Package emits: onStepChange · onRedirectRequired · onError

Migration Strategy
The migration should be incremental, not a big-bang rewrite.
Phase 1: Discovery & Contract Definition

e migration should be incremental, not a big-bang rewrite.
Phase 1: Discovery & Contract Definition
•Map every KYC step and API dependency
•List all current request/response shapes
•Define stable package interfaces
•Identify project-specific dependencies
Phase 2: Extract Shared Foundations
•Constants, types, validators
•Payload builders & utility functions
Phase 3: Extract Flow Engine
•Current step state, next/previous rules
•Resume logic, rejected/review handling
•Redirect callback handling
Phase 4: Extract API Layer
•Email OTP, Mobile OTP, KRA verification
•DigiLocker, Personal details, Nominee details
•Bank verification, Document upload, Subscription
•Selfie upload, E-sign
Phase 5: Integrate With capture-remap
•Replace local business logic with package hooks
•Keep existing UI where possible
•Wire routes to the package step state
Phase 6: QA & Stabilization

•Keep existing UI where possible
•Wire routes to the package step state
Phase 6: QA & Stabilization
•Verify happy paths, rejected/resume flows
•Verify redirect return flows
•Test bank verification retries
•Test AA / manual fallback
Suggested POC Scope
For the first POC, focus on a limited but representative scope:
•Email OTP
•Mobile OTP
•KRA verification

•Personal information
Why this scope:
•Simplest core journey
•Lower dependency on third-party redirects
•Enough to prove reusability
•Good balance between effort and value
Later stages (bank retries, file uploads, DigiLocker redirect, E-sign, AA polling) carry higher complexity
and risk — migrate those after core step success.
Risks & Mitigation
Technical Risks
•Existing logic is highly coupled and may need refactoring before extraction
•Request/response contracts may differ slightly between products

need refactoring before extraction
•Request/response contracts may differ slightly between products
•Redirect flows may behave differently across environments
•Some UI components may assume Redux-specific state shape
Delivery Risks
•Trying to move full KYC at once may delay delivery
•Insufficient test coverage may cause regressions in resume/rejected flows
Mitigation
•Start with a limited POC scope
•Use adapters for app-specific dependencies
•Keep UI unchanged in capture-remap during first integration where possible
•Test each step independently before end-to-end rollout
•Migrate complex flows after core step success
Deliverables for POC
1
Reusable KYC package scaffold in capture-core
2
Documented package API
3
Initial migrated flow for selected steps
4
Integration in capture-remap
5
Validation checklist
6
Gap list for phase 2 expansion
Success Criteria

tegration in capture-remap
5
Validation checklist
6
Gap list for phase 2 expansion
Success Criteria
The POC will be considered successful if:

•capture-remap uses KYC logic from capture-core
•No duplicate business logic is needed for selected steps
•UI remains fully controllable by consuming app
•Step transitions work correctly
•API contract remains stable and understandable
•Architecture is ready for remaining KYC steps
Recommendation
Proceed with a phased POC in capture-core using a headless KYC package design. Do not package
the full current UI from stocko-web-app directly.
•Centralize logic in capture-core
•Keep UI in capture-remap
•Use adapters for routing, modal, toast, analytics, and storage
This approach best matches the current multi-repo setup and reduces long-term duplication.
